Extract the zip and place \u003ccode\u003ertk.exe\u003c/code\u003e somewhere in your PATH (e.g. \u003ccode\u003eC:\\Users\\\u0026lt;you\u0026gt;\\.local\\bin\u003c/code\u003e). Run RTK from \u003cstrong\u003eCommand Prompt\u003c/strong\u003e, \u003cstrong\u003ePowerShell\u003c/strong\u003e, or \u003cstrong\u003eWindows Terminal\u003c/strong\u003e — do not double-click the \u003ccode\u003e.exe\u003c/code\u003e (it will flash and close). The full hook system works natively on Windows (and in \u003ca href=\"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install\" rel=\"nofollow\"\u003eWSL\u003c/a\u003e). Install for your AI tool\nrtk init -g                     # Claude Code / Copilot (default)\nrtk init -g --gemini            # Gemini CLI\nrtk init -g --codex             # Codex (OpenAI)\nrtk init -g --agent cursor      # Cursor\nrtk init -g --agent windsurf    # Windsurf\nrtk init --agent cline          # Cline / Roo Code\nrtk init --agent kilocode       # Kilo Code\nrtk init --agent antigravity    # Google Antigravity\nrtk init -g --agent pi          # Pi\nrtk init --agent hermes         # Hermes\n\n# 2. Restart your AI tool, then test\ngit status  # Automatically rewritten to rtk git status\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e 1. Install for your AI tool\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init -g                     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Claude Code / Copilot (default)\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init -g --gemini            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Gemini CLI\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init -g --codex             \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Codex (OpenAI)\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init -g --agent cursor      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Cursor\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init -g --agent windsurf    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Windsurf\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init --agent cline          \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Cline / Roo Code\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init --agent kilocode       \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Kilo Code\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init --agent antigravity    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Google Antigravity\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init -g --agent pi          \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Pi\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init --agent hermes         \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Hermes\u003c/span\u003e\n\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e 2. Restart your AI tool, then test\u003c/span\u003e\ngit status  \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Automatically rewritten to rtk git status\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eHook-based agents rewrite Bash commands (e.g., \u003ccode\u003egit status\u003c/code\u003e -\u0026gt; \u003ccode\u003ertk git status\u003c/code\u003e) before execution. Plugin-based agents, including Hermes, use their plugin API to rewrite commands before execution. The agent receives compact output without needing to call \u003ccode\u003ertk\u003c/code\u003e explicitly.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eImportant:\u003c/strong\u003e the hook only runs on Bash tool calls. Claude Code built-in tools like \u003ccode\u003eRead\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eGrep\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode\u003eGlob\u003c/code\u003e do not pass through the Bash hook, so they are not auto-rewritten. To get RTK's compact output for those workflows, use shell commands (\u003ccode\u003ecat\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003ehead\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003etail\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003erg\u003c/code\u003e/\u003ccode\u003egrep\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003efind\u003c/code\u003e) or call \u003ccode\u003ertk read\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003ertk grep\u003c/code\u003e, or \u003ccode\u003ertk find\u003c/code\u003e directly.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" Claude Code built-in tools such as \u003ccode\u003eRead\u003c/code\u003e, \u003ccode\u003eGrep\u003c/code\u003e, and \u003ccode\u003eGlob\u003c/code\u003e bypass the hook, so use shell commands or explicit \u003ccode\u003ertk\u003c/code\u003e commands when you want RTK filtering there.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" Download and extract rtk-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip from releases\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e 2. Add rtk.exe to your PATH (e.g. C:\\Users\\\u0026lt;you\u0026gt;\\.local\\bin)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e 3. Initialize — installs the native binary hook\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk init \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003e-\u003c/span\u003eg\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eUpgrading from an older install?\u003c/strong\u003e If you set RTK up before v0.37.2 you may still have the legacy \u003ccode\u003ertk-rewrite.sh\u003c/code\u003e shell hook (which does need a Unix shell). Re-run \u003ccode\u003ertk init -g\u003c/code\u003e to migrate to the native binary hook.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003ePrerequisites\u003c/strong\u003e: some filters shell out to \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep\"\u003eripgrep\u003c/a\u003e (\u003ccode\u003erg\u003c/code\u003e). Install it and keep it on your PATH (e.g. \u003ccode\u003ewinget install BurntSushi.ripgrep.MSVC\u003c/code\u003e) to avoid \u003ccode\u003eBinary 'rg' not found on PATH\u003c/code\u003e warnings.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eImportant\u003c/strong\u003e: Do not double-click \u003ccode\u003ertk.exe\u003c/code\u003e — it is a CLI tool that prints usage and exits immediately. Always run it from a terminal (Command Prompt, PowerShell, or Windows Terminal).\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003ch3 tabindex=\"-1\" binary)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGitHub Copilot (VS Code)\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ertk init -g --copilot\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ePreToolUse hook — transparent rewrite\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGitHub Copilot CLI\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ertk init -g --copilot\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ePreToolUse deny-with-suggestion (CLI limitation)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eCursor\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003ccode\u003ertk init -g --agent cursor\u003c/code\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003epreToolUse hook (hooks.json)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eGemini collect \u003cstrong\u003eanonymous, aggregate usage metrics\u003c/strong\u003e once per day. Telemetry is \u003cstrong\u003edisabled by default\u003c/strong\u003e and requires \u003cstrong\u003eexplicit opt-in consent\u003c/strong\u003e (GDPR Art. 6, 7) during \u003ccode\u003ertk init\u003c/code\u003e or via \u003ccode\u003ertk telemetry enable\u003c/code\u003e. This data helps us build a better product: identifying which commands need filters, which filters need improvement, and how much value RTK delivers. For the full list of fields, data handling, and contributor guidelines, see \u003cstrong\u003e\u003ca href=\"/rtk-ai/rtk/blob/develop/docs/TELEMETRY.md\"\u003edocs/TELEMETRY.md\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat is collected and why:\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cmarkdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003cthead\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eCategory\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eData\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003cth\u003eWhy\u003c/th\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/thead\u003e\n\u003ctbody\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eIdentity\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eSalted device hash (SHA-256, not reversible)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCount unique installations without tracking individuals\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEnvironment\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRTK version, OS, architecture, install method\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eKnow which platforms to support and test\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUsage volume\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCommand count (24h), total commands, tokens saved (24h/30d/total)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eMeasure adoption and value delivered\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eQuality\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eTop 5 passthrough commands (0% savings), parse failure count, commands with \u0026lt;30% savings\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eIdentify missing filters and weak ones to improve\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEcosystem\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eCommand category distribution (e.g. git 45%, cargo 20%, js 15%)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003ePrioritize filter development for popular ecosystems\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eRetention\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eDays since first use, active days in last 30\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUnderstand engagement and detect churn\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAdoption\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eAI agent hook type (claude/gemini/codex), custom TOML filter count\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eTrack integration coverage and DSL adoption\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eConfiguration\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eWhether config.toml exists, number of excluded commands, project count\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUnderstand user maturity and customization patterns\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eFeatures\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eUsage counts for meta-commands (gain, discover, proxy, verify)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eKnow which RTK features are valued vs unused\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEconomics\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eEstimated USD savings (based on API token pricing)\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd\u003eQuantify the value RTK provides to users\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/tbody\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\u003c/markdown-accessiblity-table\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003eAll data is \u003cstrong\u003eaggregate counts or anonymized command names\u003c/strong\u003e (first 3 words, no arguments). Top commands report only tool names (e.g. \"git\", \"cargo\"), never full command lines.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhat is NOT collected:\u003c/strong\u003e source code, file paths, command arguments, secrets, environment variables, personal data, or repository contents.\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eManage telemetry:\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"rtk telemetry status     # Check current consent state\nrtk telemetry enable     # Give consent (interactive prompt)\nrtk telemetry disable    # Withdraw consent — stops all collection immediately\nrtk telemetry forget     # Withdraw consent + delete all local data + request server-side erasure\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003ertk telemetry status     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Check current consent state\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk telemetry \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eenable\u003c/span\u003e     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Give consent (interactive prompt)\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk telemetry disable    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Withdraw consent — stops all collection immediately\u003c/span\u003e\nrtk telemetry forget     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e#\u003c/span\u003e Withdraw consent + delete all local data + request server-side erasure\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/pre\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n\u003cp dir=\"auto\"\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eOverride via handle exit code 3 from rtk rewrite

Interpret all RTK rewrite exit codes and respond appropriately:
- Exit 0 (Allow): auto-apply rewrite
- Exit 1 (Passthrough): no RTK equivalent, pass through unchanged
- Exit 2 (Deny): block the call entirely
- Exit 3 (Ask): rewrite available, require user approval via
  requireApproval with allow-once/deny decisions

Uses execFileSync (not execSync) to avoid shell injection. Returns
a [string | null, RewriteVerdict?] tuple from tryRewrite so the
before_tool_call hook can react to each verdict type.

"allow-always" omitted from allowedDecisions because OpenClaw does
not auto-persist approval for plugin hooks — see:
https://docs.openclaw.ai/plugins/plugin-permission-requests#troubleshooting

Closes #2202" class="Link--secondary" href="/rtk-ai/rtk/commit/487a2e7b2c381071e61062e5dfff8d2e790c54c8">fix(openclaw): handle exit code 3 from rtk rewrite</a></div></div></td><td><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix(ci): pin fixture line endings, harden CRLF tests

test (windows-latest) failed on the two CRLF tests: with no
.gitattributes, the Windows runner's core.autocrlf=true checks fixtures
out with CRLF, include_str! embeds \r\n, and the tests'
replace('\n', "\r\n") synthesis produces \r\r\n. str::lines() strips
only the final \r\n of each pair, leaving a stray \r that $-anchored
regexes (MODULE_BANNER, BUILD_FOOT) reject — dropping the module banner
and BUILD SUCCESS in the CRLF leg only. Real Maven CRLF output (single
\r\n) was always handled; only the synthesized double-CR leg broke.

Root-cause fix: tests/fixtures/** -text — fixtures are byte-exact
filter inputs; eol conversion must never touch them. Defense-in-depth:
the two CRLF tests normalize the embedded fixture back to LF before
synthesizing, so they stay correct even on checkouts without
attributes (zip downloads, pre-existing autocrlf clones).

Also fixes the test doc comment, which claimed str::lines() keeps the
trailing \r of a \r\n pair — it strips it; the hazard is the doubled
\r\r\n, not single CRLF." class="Link--secondary" href="/rtk-ai/rtk/commit/be28a511797fb5214ff0784f57f491d2b7dd0e71">fix(ci): pin fixture line endings, harden CRLF tests</a></div></div></td><td><div data-discover="true">.gitignore</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="feat(benchmark): add multipass VM integration test suite

Bun/TypeScript orchestrator that creates an Ubuntu 24.04 VM via multipass,
installs all dev tools (Rust, Go, Node, Python, .NET, Terraform, etc.),
builds RTK, and runs 103 tests across 11 phases:

- Cargo quality (fmt, clippy, test)
- 47 Rust built-in commands (git, ls, grep, cargo, pytest, go, tsc...)
- 21 TOML filter commands (df, ps, shellcheck, hadolint, helm...)
- Hook rewrite engine (17 rewrite assertions)
- Exit code preservation
- Token savings verification (avg 81%)
- Pipe compatibility
- Edge cases (unicode, ANSI, empty output)
- Performance (memory < 20MB)
- Concurrency (10 parallel executions)

Usage:
  bun run scripts/benchmark/run.ts           # Full suite (~3 min)
  bun run scripts/benchmark/run.ts --quick   # Skip perf/concurrency
  bun run scripts/benchmark/run.ts --phase 3 # Single phase
  bun run scripts/benchmark/cleanup.ts       # Delete VM
  bun run scripts/benchmark/rebuild.ts       # Fast rebuild

Prerequisites: brew install multipass, bun
Signed-off-by: Patrick szymkowiak <patrick.szymkowiak@innovtech.eu>" class="Link--secondary" href="/rtk-ai/rtk/commit/d22759b8c5254ad9c4a455f10cb7de75e92df581">feat(benchmark): add multipass VM data-discover="true">CONTRIBUTING.md</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class="react-directory-row-commit-cell"><div><div class="react-directory-commit-message"><a data-pjax="true" title="fix(core): never-worse output guard so RTK never exceeds the raw command

RTK could emit more tokens than the underlying command on small inputs:
filters that add headers, summaries, re-indentation, or a tee hint, plus
synthetic no-result messages ("0 matches", "No stashes", "[docker] 0
containers") printed where the raw command emitted nothing. Both break the
Transparency principle and inflate tokens instead of saving them.

- core::guard::never_worse(raw, filtered) returns raw when the filtered form
  has more tokens (reuses tracking::estimate_tokens), so RTK output is never
  larger than the real command.
- runner::emit_guarded(filtered, hint, raw) composes body + tee hint, guards
  the whole, prints, and returns what was shown so printed == tracked.
- run_captured_filter guards the run_filtered* family centrally; per-site
  guards cover the remaining single-string filters.
- On empty raw, emit empty and preserve the exit code instead of a synthetic
  no-result message (the messages were cosmetic with no dependents; #2461
  reports the grep one as actively harmful).
- git stash show now propagates its exit code instead of masking a real
  failure as Ok(0).

Resolves #2551." class="Link--secondary" href="/rtk-ai/rtk/commit/861a46dee57f50216862ac83b0ee57974f383203">fix(core): never-worse output guard so RTK never exceeds the raw command</a></div></div></td><td><div class="react-directory-commit-age"><relative-time data-component="RelativeTime" tense="past" datetime="2026-06-23T19:53:35.000Z" title="Jun 23, 2026, 7:53 PM document native binary hook support

Native Windows has had full auto-rewrite support since v0.37.2 via the
`rtk hook claude` native binary command (no bash/jq/Unix shell). The
README still described the pre-v0.37.2 "CLAUDE.md fallback" behavior,
which is now inaccurate and a recurring source of confusion (#330).

- Rewrite the Windows section: native binary hook is the primary path,
  WSL secondary; drop the "limited support / CLAUDE.md fallback" framing
- Add an upgrade note (re-run `rtk init -g` to migrate off rtk-rewrite.sh)
- Add a ripgrep prerequisite note (`rg` on PATH)
- Fix the install note and Supported AI Tools table (bash -> native binary)

Refs #330

title="fix: patrick's 3 issues (latest tag, ccusage fallback, versioning)

1. CRITICAL: Fix 'latest' tag creation after releases
   - Move update-latest-tag job from release.yml to release-please.yml
   - release-please creates tags via API (no push event) → must run in same workflow
   - Job now conditional on release_created output

2. IMPORTANT: Add npx fallback for ccusage + improve message
   - Check binary in PATH first, fallback to 'npx ccusage'
   - Updated message: "npm i -g ccusage (or use npx ccusage)"
   - Consistent with other JS tooling (next_cmd, tsc_cmd, prettier_cmd)

3. PROCESS: Slow down version bumps with release-please config
   - Add release-please-config.json with bump-patch-for-minor-pre-major
   - In 0.x versions: feat: → patch bump instead of minor
   - Prevents rapid version inflation (0.3.1 → 0.5.0 in 21h)

Fixes issues raised by Patrick after PR #21 merge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" class="Link--secondary" href="/rtk-ai/rtk/commit/9e322e2aee9f7239cf04ce1bf9971920035ac4bb">fix: patrick's 3 issues (latest tag, ccusage <p align="center" dir="auto">
  <strong>High-performance CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90%</strong>
</p>
<p align="center" dir="auto">

<hr>
<p dir="auto">rtk filters and compresses command outputs before they reach your LLM context. Single Rust binary, 100+ supported commands, <10ms overhead.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Token Savings (30-min Claude Code Session)</h2><a id="user-content-token-savings-30-min-claude-code-session" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Token Savings (30-min Claude Code Session)" href="#token-savings-30-min-claude-code-session"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<blockquote>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Windows users</strong>: Extract the zip and place <code>rtk.exe</code> somewhere in your PATH (e.g. <code>C:\Users\<you>\.local\bin</code>). Run RTK from <strong>Command Prompt</strong>, <strong>PowerShell</strong>, or <strong>Windows Terminal</strong> — do not double-click the <code>.exe</code> (it will flash and close). The full hook system works natively on Windows (and in <a href="https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install" rel="nofollow">WSL</a>). See <a href="#windows">Windows setup</a> below for details.</p>
</blockquote>

<p dir="auto">Hook-based agents rewrite Bash commands (e.g., <code>git status</code> -> <code>rtk git status</code>) before execution. Plugin-based agents, including Hermes, use their plugin API to rewrite commands before execution. The agent receives compact output without needing to call <code>rtk</code> explicitly.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Important:</strong> the hook only runs on Bash tool calls. Claude Code built-in tools like <code>Read</code>, <code>Grep</code>, and <code>Glob</code> do not pass through the Bash hook, so they are not auto-rewritten. To get RTK's compact output for those workflows, use shell commands (<code>cat</code>/<code>head</code>/<code>tail</code>, <code>rg</code>/<code>grep</code>, <code>find</code>) or call <code>rtk read</code>, <code>rtk grep</code>, or <code>rtk find</code> directly.</p>

<p dir="auto">Four strategies applied per command type:</p>
<ol dir="auto">
<li><strong>Smart Filtering</strong> - Removes noise (comments, whitespace, boilerplate)</li>
<li><strong>Grouping</strong> - Aggregates similar items (files by directory, errors by type)</li>
<li><strong>Truncation</strong> - Keeps relevant context, cuts redundancy</li>
<li><strong>Deduplication</strong> - Collapses repeated log lines with counts</li>
</ol>

<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>rtk ls <span class="pl-c1">.</span>                        <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Token-optimized directory tree</span>

rtk find <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>*.rs<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> <span class="pl-c1">.</span>               <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Compact find results</span>
rtk grep <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>pattern<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span> <span class="pl-c1">.</span>            <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Grouped search results</span>
rtk diff file1 file2            <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Condensed diff (exit 1 if files differ)</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Auto-Rewrite Hook</h2><a id="user-content-auto-rewrite-hook" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Auto-Rewrite Hook" href="#auto-rewrite-hook"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">The most effective way to use rtk. The hook transparently intercepts Bash commands and rewrites them to rtk equivalents before execution.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Result</strong>: 100% rtk adoption across all conversations and subagents, zero token overhead.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Scope note:</strong> this only applies to Bash tool calls. Claude Code built-in tools such as <code>Read</code>, <code>Grep</code>, and <code>Glob</code> bypass the hook, so use shell commands or explicit <code>rtk</code> commands when you want RTK filtering there.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Setup</h3><a id="user-content-setup" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Setup" href="#setup"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>rtk init -g                 <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Install hook + RTK.md (recommended)</span>
rtk init -g --opencode      <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> OpenCode plugin (instead of Claude Code)</span>
rtk init -g --auto-patch    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Non-interactive (CI/CD)</span>
rtk init -g --hook-only     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Hook only, no RTK.md</span>
rtk init --show             <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Verify installation</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

rtk init -g --auto-patch    # Non-interactive (CI/CD)
rtk init -g --hook-only     # Hook only, no RTK.md
rtk init --show             # Verify installation" tabindex="0" role="button">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Windows</h2><a id="user-content-windows" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Windows" href="#windows"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">RTK works fully on native Windows. Since <strong>v0.37.2</strong> the auto-rewrite hook runs as a <strong>native binary command</strong> (<code>rtk hook claude</code>) — no Unix shell, bash, or jq required — so commands are rewritten transparently on Command Prompt, PowerShell, and Windows Terminal, just like on Linux and macOS.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Native Windows</h3><a id="user-content-native-windows" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Native Windows" href="#native-windows"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-powershell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> 1. Download and extract rtk-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip from releases</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> 2. Add rtk.exe to your PATH (e.g. C:\Users\<you>\.local\bin)</span>
<span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> 3. Initialize — installs the native binary hook</span>
rtk init <span class="pl-k">-</span>g</pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="# 1. # 2. Add rtk.exe to your PATH (e.g. C:\Users\<you>\.local\bin)
# 3. Initialize — installs the native binary hook
rtk init -g" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto"><strong>Upgrading from an older install?</strong> If you set RTK up before v0.37.2 you may still have the legacy <code>rtk-rewrite.sh</code> shell hook (which does need a Unix shell). Re-run <code>rtk init -g</code> to migrate to the native binary hook.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Prerequisites</strong>: some filters shell out to <a href="https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep">ripgrep</a> (<code>rg</code>). Install it and keep it on your PATH (e.g. <code>winget install BurntSushi.ripgrep.MSVC</code>) to avoid <code>Binary 'rg' not found on PATH</code> warnings.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Important</strong>: Do not double-click <code>rtk.exe</code> — it is a CLI tool that prints usage and exits immediately. Always run it from a terminal (Command Prompt, PowerShell, or Windows Terminal).</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h3 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">WSL</h3><a id="user-content-wsl" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: WSL" href="#wsl"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Supported AI Tools</h2><a id="user-content-supported-ai-tools" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Supported AI Tools" href="#supported-ai-tools"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">RTK supports 14 AI coding tools. Each integration rewrites shell commands to <code>rtk</code> equivalents for 60-90% token savings where the agent supports command interception.</p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Tool</th>
<th>Install</th>
<th>Method</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Claude Code</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g</code></td>
<td>PreToolUse hook (native binary)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>GitHub Copilot (VS Code)</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --copilot</code></td>
<td>PreToolUse hook — transparent rewrite</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>GitHub Copilot CLI</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --copilot</code></td>
<td>PreToolUse deny-with-suggestion (CLI limitation)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Cursor</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --agent cursor</code></td>
<td>preToolUse hook (hooks.json)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Gemini CLI</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --gemini</code></td>
<td>BeforeTool hook</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Codex</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --codex</code></td>
<td>AGENTS.md + RTK.md instructions</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Windsurf</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --agent windsurf</code></td>
<td>.windsurfrules (project-scoped)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Cline / Roo Code</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init --agent cline</code></td>
<td>.clinerules (project-scoped)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>OpenCode</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --opencode</code></td>
<td>Plugin TS (tool.execute.before)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>OpenClaw</strong></td>
<td><code>openclaw plugins install ./openclaw</code></td>
<td>Plugin TS (before_tool_call)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Pi</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init -g --agent pi</code> (global)</td>
<td>TypeScript extension (tool_call)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Hermes</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init --agent hermes</code></td>
<td>Python plugin adapter (terminal command mutation via <code>rtk rewrite</code>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Mistral Vibe</strong></td>
<td>Planned (<a href="https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/issues/800" data-hovercard-type="issue" data-hovercard-url="/rtk-ai/rtk/issues/800/hovercard" aria-keyshortcuts="Alt+ArrowUp">#800</a>)</td>
<td>Blocked on upstream</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Kilo Code</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init --agent kilocode</code></td>
<td>.kilocode/rules/rtk-rules.md (project-scoped)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Google Antigravity</strong></td>
<td><code>rtk init --agent antigravity</code></td>
<td>.agents/rules/antigravity-rtk-rules.md (project-scoped)</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">For per-agent setup details, override controls, and graceful degradation, see the <a href="https://www.rtk-ai.app/guide/getting-started/supported-agents" rel="nofollow">Supported Agents guide</a>. The Hermes plugin source and tests live in <code>hooks/hermes/</code>; installed Hermes runtime files still live under <code>~/.hermes/plugins/rtk-rewrite/</code>.</p>
<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Configuration</h2><a id="user-content-configuration" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Configuration" href="#configuration"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto"><code>~/.config/rtk/config.toml</code> (macOS: <code>~/Library/Application Support/rtk/config.toml</code>):</p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-toml notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>[<span class="pl-en">hooks</span>]
<span class="pl-smi">exclude_commands</span> = [<span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>curl<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>, <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>playwright<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>]  <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> skip rewrite for these</span>

[<span class="pl-en">tee</span>]
<span class="pl-smi">enabled</span> = <span class="pl-c1">true</span>          <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> save raw output on failure (default: true)</span>
<span class="pl-smi">mode</span> = <span class="pl-s"><span class="pl-pds">"</span>failures<span class="pl-pds">"</span></span>       <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> "failures", "always", or "never"</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="[hooks]
exclude_commands = ["curl", "playwright"]  # skip rewrite for these

[tee]
enabled = true          # save raw output on failure (default: true)
mode = "failures"       # "failures", "always", or "never"" tabindex="0" role="button">

<p dir="auto">When a command fails, RTK saves the full unfiltered output so the LLM can read it without re-executing:</p>

</code></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto"><h2 tabindex="-1" class="heading-element" dir="auto">Privacy & Telemetry</h2><a id="user-content-privacy--telemetry" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Privacy & Telemetry" href="#privacy--telemetry"><svg data-component="Octicon" class="octicon octicon-link" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" height="16" aria-hidden="true"><path d="m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z"></path></svg></a></div>
<p dir="auto">RTK can collect <strong>anonymous, aggregate usage metrics</strong> once per day. Telemetry is <strong>disabled by default</strong> and requires <strong>explicit opt-in consent</strong> (GDPR Art. 6, 7) during <code>rtk init</code> or via <code>rtk telemetry enable</code>. This data helps us build a better product: identifying which commands need filters, which filters need improvement, and how much value RTK delivers. For the full list of fields, data handling, and contributor guidelines, see <strong><a href="/rtk-ai/rtk/blob/develop/docs/TELEMETRY.md">docs/TELEMETRY.md</a></strong>.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What is collected and why:</strong></p>
<markdown-accessiblity-table data-catalyst=""><table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>Category</th>
<th>Data</th>
<th>Why</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Identity</td>
<td>Salted device hash (SHA-256, not reversible)</td>
<td>Count unique installations without tracking individuals</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Environment</td>
<td>RTK version, OS, architecture, install method</td>
<td>Know which platforms to support and test</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Usage volume</td>
<td>Command count (24h), total commands, tokens saved (24h/30d/total)</td>
<td>Measure adoption and value delivered</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Quality</td>
<td>Top 5 passthrough commands (0% savings), parse failure count, commands with <30% savings</td>
<td>Identify missing filters and weak ones to improve</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Ecosystem</td>
<td>Command category distribution (e.g. git 45%, cargo 20%, js 15%)</td>
<td>Prioritize filter development for popular ecosystems</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Retention</td>
<td>Days since first use, active days in last 30</td>
<td>Understand engagement and detect churn</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Adoption</td>
<td>AI agent hook type (claude/gemini/codex), custom TOML filter count</td>
<td>Track integration coverage and DSL adoption</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Configuration</td>
<td>Whether config.toml exists, number of excluded commands, project count</td>
<td>Understand user maturity and customization patterns</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Features</td>
<td>Usage counts for meta-commands (gain, discover, proxy, verify)</td>
<td>Know which RTK features are valued vs unused</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Economics</td>
<td>Estimated USD savings (based on API token pricing)</td>
<td>Quantify the value RTK provides to users</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>
<p dir="auto">All data is <strong>aggregate counts or anonymized command names</strong> (first 3 words, no arguments). Top commands report only tool names (e.g. "git", "cargo"), never full command lines.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>What is NOT collected:</strong> source code, file paths, command arguments, secrets, environment variables, personal data, or repository contents.</p>
<p dir="auto"><strong>Manage telemetry:</strong></p>
<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre>rtk telemetry status     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Check current consent state</span>
rtk telemetry <span class="pl-c1">enable</span>     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Give consent (interactive prompt)</span>
rtk telemetry disable    <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Withdraw consent — stops all collection immediately</span>
rtk telemetry forget     <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Withdraw consent + delete all local data + request server-side erasure</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">
    <clipboard-copy aria-label="Copy code to clipboard" class="ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center" data-copy-feedback="Copied!" data-tooltip-direction="w" value="rtk telemetry status     # Check current consent state
rtk telemetry enable     # Give consent (interactive prompt)
rtk telemetry disable    # Withdraw consent — stops all collection immediately
rtk telemetry forget     # Withdraw consent + delete all local data + request server-side erasure" tabindex="0" role="button">
      <svg aria-hidden="true" data-component="Octicon" height="16" viewBox="0 0 16 16" version="1.1" width="16" data-view-component="true" class="octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon">

<div class="highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" dir="auto"><pre><span class="pl-k">export</span> RTK_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1   <span class="pl-c"><span class="pl-c">#</span> Blocks telemetry regardless of consent</span></pre><div class="zeroclipboard-container">

<div class="BorderGrid ">
  <div class="BorderGrid-row">
    <div class="BorderGrid-cell">
      <div class="hide-sm hide-md">
  <h2 class="tmp-mb-3 h4">About</h2>

      <p class="f4 tmp-my-3">
        CLI proxy that reduces LLM token consumption by 60-90% on common dev commands. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies
      </p>
